summary: Tested dual payment gateway integration (PayFast + Stitch) on Cape Ember Coffee. Backend: 10/11 tests pass. Frontend: Checkout page correctly displays both payment options with working selection. CRITICAL ISSUE: Stitch token endpoint returns 'invalid_client' for the supplied client_credentials -> /api/stitch/create-payment returns 500. PayFast end-to-end works (form fields + signature generated). Order creation via /api/orders works for both methods.

critical.issue: Stitch OAuth token request to https://secure.stitch.money/connect/token returns 400 invalid_client. Backend logs: "Stitch token error: {\"error\":\"invalid_client\"}". Endpoint then returns 500 "Failed to create Stitch payment". Root cause: Stitch's OAuth2 client_credentials flow does NOT accept a plain client_secret. It requires a JWT client_assertion (private_key_jwt) signed with a private key registered in the Stitch dashboard, with params client_assertion_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:client-assertion-type:jwt-bearer and client_assertion=<signed JWT>. Current implementation in get_stitch_access_token() in /app/backend/server.py (lines 595-614) sends grant_type=client_credentials + client_id + client_secret which Stitch rejects.

minor.issue: Order is created with status PENDING even when subsequent Stitch payment creation fails. Orphan orders accumulate. Consider rolling back the order or marking it failed if payment creation throws.
Cart is NOT cleared after order creation. After /api/orders returns success, the same cart items remain and a duplicate order can be created on retry. Frontend cart still shows the items.

create_stitch_payment() in server.py (lines 616-667) does not pass return/cancel URLs to the Stitch mutation input. The redirect_url argument is accepted but never used; Stitch needs successUrl/cancelUrl/failureUrl in input for redirect-after-payment.

ui_bugs.issue: When /checkout is navigated to directly (not via Cart -> Proceed), CartContext is briefly empty and the component calls navigate('/cart') during render, triggering React warning: 'Cannot update a component (BrowserRouter) while rendering a different component (CheckoutPage)'. Move the redirect into useEffect.

integration_issues.issue: Clicking 'Pay with Stitch' will fail end-to-end because /api/stitch/create-payment returns 500. User sees 'Checkout failed. Please try again.' Frontend wiring is correct; backend integration is broken.

test_evidence.checkout_ui: Both payment-payfast and payment-stitch options render with correct icons and descriptions. Radio selection switches the Pay button label between 'Pay with PayFast' and 'Pay with Stitch'.
test_evidence.payfast_api: Returns payfast_host=www.payfast.co.za and full fields dict including 32-char md5 signature, m_payment_id matches order_id.
test_evidence.orders_api: Creates orders with both 'payfast' and 'stitch' payment_method; returns order_id, order_number (CE-NNNNNN), total, and whatsapp_link.
test_evidence.stitch_api: Fails with 500 due to upstream Stitch OAuth invalid_client error.

action_items: [CRITICAL] Rewrite get_stitch_access_token() to use private_key_jwt client assertion as required by Stitch. Need a PRIVATE KEY (PEM) registered in Stitch dashboard, not just a client secret. Reference: https://docs.stitch.money/docs/payments-api-authentication. The provided STITCH_CLIENT_SECRET is most likely meant to be a private key or the user has not yet registered a key pair with Stitch. Ask user to confirm whether they have a registered key pair, otherwise the integration cannot work.
[CRITICAL] Pass successUrl/cancelUrl/failureUrl in the clientPaymentInitiationRequestCreate GraphQL input so users return to the order success page after payment.
[MEDIUM] Move CheckoutPage's empty-cart redirect into useEffect to remove React warning and avoid the brief 'navigate during render' issue.
[MEDIUM] Clear the user's cart after /api/orders successfully creates the order (or after webhook confirms payment).
[LOW] Roll back/mark-failed the order if Stitch payment creation fails so we don't accumulate PENDING orders.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 2393 lines - exceeds 700-line guideline. Split into routers (auth, products, cart, orders, payments, webhooks).
get_stitch_access_token() uses wrong OAuth grant for Stitch (client_secret instead of private_key_jwt).
create_stitch_payment() ignores its redirect_url parameter - URLs not actually sent to Stitch.
/api/orders does not clear cart after successful order creation - leads to duplicate orders.
CheckoutPage.js calls navigate() inside render (lines 109-112) instead of useEffect - React strict-mode warning.
Hard-coded PayFast default 'Western Cape' shipping (R65) in /api/orders does not honor the address province - inconsistent with /api/checkout which calls calculate_shipping().
verify_stitch_webhook returns True when STITCH_WEBHOOK_SECRET is empty - security risk in production (it is currently empty in .env).

success_rate.backend: 91% (10/11 tests pass; Stitch payment creation fails)
success_rate.frontend: 100% UI (dual options visible, selection works); 50% integration (PayFast works, Stitch will fail at runtime)

seed_data_creation: Added 1x Fynbos Roast 250g whole bean to the test user's cart via /api/cart/add for checkout testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend dual-payment UI is fully working (PayFast and Stitch radio options + dynamic Pay button label). PayFast backend flow is fully working end-to-end (form fields + valid md5 signature). The blocker is Stitch authentication: Stitch's OAuth endpoint rejects client_credentials with plain client_secret and requires private_key_jwt client assertion. Without a registered key pair in the Stitch dashboard, the Stitch integration cannot work. To retest after fix: run pytest /app/backend/tests/test_checkout_payments.py and ensure TestStitchCreatePayment::test_stitch_returns_redirect_url returns 200 with a stitch.money URL.
rca of the issue: Stitch's /connect/token endpoint requires JWT-based client authentication (private_key_jwt), not basic client_secret. Backend code in get_stitch_access_token() posts grant_type=client_credentials with client_id + client_secret which Stitch rejects with HTTP 400 invalid_client. The endpoint then surfaces as HTTP 500 to the frontend. Reproduction: POST /api/stitch/create-payment with a valid order_id returns 500 'Failed to create Stitch payment'. Backend log shows: 'Stitch token error: {"error":"invalid_client"}'. Mitigation: Switch to private_key_jwt - sign a JWT with iss=client_id, sub=client_id, aud=https://secure.stitch.money/connect/token, exp <=300s, then POST with client_assertion_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:client-assertion-type:jwt-bearer and client_assertion=<signedJWT>. The user must register the corresponding public key in the Stitch dashboard.
